prompt:
Weekly AI model pricing refresh (run from repo root /workspace/ninja). Run TWO scrapers IN ORDER:
1) python apps/model-pricing-dashboard/scrape_pricing.py — scrapes the public aggregator aipricing.guru via Tavily and regenerates apps/model-pricing-dashboard/data/models.json with a fresh last_collected timestamp. It aborts on 0 rows; unmatched models keep their last-known price flagged price_stale.
2) python apps/model-pricing-dashboard/scrape_official.py — drives the persistent browser skill to render each providers OWN pricing page and overlays confirmed "official" prices on top of the aggregator baseline. MUST run AFTER step 1 so official prices take precedence. It matches strictly by exact model name and leaves any model it cannot confirm untouched.
The repo auto-commits the change. Then post ONE short summary line to Slack: official matched count (see the official_refresh block / script stdout) + aggregator matched vs stale counts + the new last_collected date. If either scraper reports 0 rows or errors, say so and do NOT overwrite data on failure (scrape_pricing aborts on 0 rows; scrape_official leaves prices untouched if a page cannot be rendered). Public sources only; no API keys, no paid logins.
